title: Rei Lilith as a God at the end of EOE
display_title: Rei Lilith as a God at the end of EOE
wikitext:
[]: Rei/Lilith as a God at the end of EOE?             Quote Post by TemporalKnight » Sat Nov 10, 2007 5:00 pm So I was just rewatching EOE a few days ago and I got to thinking some strange thoughts. So I came back here to research a few things and try to remember all of the crazy things that have been discussed. My basic question/reasoning is this: So the Spear of Longinus is supposed to stop the SoL's from attaining god-like powers right? Well it for some inscrutable reason decides not to stop Shogouki from reaching her god-like status and instead merges with her to form the ToL. Then obviously GNR comes and absorbs that and starts/ends 3I. Even after Sho comes back out of GNR the LoL (sorry I can't remember the abbreviation for it) does nothing except destroy the copy Spears. GNR goes on to psychically die while her soul lives on. I just recently read a thread where someone mentioned that Rei 3 might have transcended after Lilith's "death" hence why she is at both the beginning and end, and able to talk to Shinji while he is trapped inside the Eva. Why can't we take this one step further though? Why can't we say that Lilith/Rei 3 has actually attained TRUE Godhood, not just the amazing uber powers of Instrumentality-ness but the status that the FAR specifically tried to prevent their SoL's from attaining? What if Lilith/Rei 3 is now actually as powerful if not more so then her creators? What if she is a god now? If this has already been discussed I'm sorry but I just needed to ask it and see what others think. I'm not arrogant enough to consider myself an expert and I'd rather ask people who have studied this far longer than myself.

[]: Re: Rei/Lilith as a God at the end of EOE?             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Nov 10, 2007 5:13 pm TemporalKnight wrote: So I was just rewatching EOE a few days ago and I got to thinking some strange thoughts. So I came back here to research a few things and try to remember all of the crazy things that have been discussed. My basic question/reasoning is this: So the Spear of Longinus is supposed to stop the SoL's from attaining god-like powers right? Well it for some inscrutable reason decides not to stop Shogouki from reaching her god-like status and instead merges with her to form the ToL. Then obviously GNR comes and absorbs that and starts/ends 3I. Even after Sho comes back out of GNR the LoL (sorry I can't remember the abbreviation for it) does nothing except destroy the copy Spears. GNR goes on to psychically die while her soul lives on. I just recently read a thread where someone mentioned that Rei 3 might have transcended after Lilith's "death" hence why she is at both the beginning and end, and able to talk to Shinji while he is trapped inside the Eva. Why can't we take this one step further though? Why can't we say that Lilith/Rei 3 has actually attained TRUE Godhood, not just the amazing uber powers of Instrumentality-ness but the status that the FAR specifically tried to prevent their SoL's from attaining? What if Lilith/Rei 3 is now actually as powerful if not more so then her creators? What if she is a god now? If this has already been discussed I'm sorry but I just needed to ask it and see what others think. I'm not arrogant enough to consider myself an expert and I'd rather ask people who have studied this far longer than myself.

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Sat Nov 10, 2007 5:47 pm It's an interesting theory, but I have to go with Alaska Slim here. Seele goes on about Eva 01 and how, after it ingested the S^2 Engine it became like a god. LIKE a god is not A god. The case is the same for Lillith/GNR/whatever want to call her.

[]:             Quote Post by TemporalKnight » Sat Nov 10, 2007 6:49 pm I didn't mean a GOD, god but more like a god as in how the people in Eva think of the FAR (if they even know about them that is considering they're in the game rather than the anime). But anyways I'm not attempting to imply that Rei became omniscient, though it would seem that she has some rather interesting new abilities (such as existing outside of time).

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Nov 10, 2007 7:12 pm GNRei/Shekinah has the qualities of a transhuman being -- most closely akin to the SysOp in some Singularitarian writings.

[]: Re: Rei/Lilith as a God at the end of EOE?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Nov 10, 2007 9:26 pm TemporalKnight wrote: So the Spear of Longinus is supposed to stop the SoL's from attaining god-like powers right? Well it for some inscrutable reason decides not to stop Shogouki from reaching her god-like status and instead merges with her to form the ToL.

[]: Re: Rei/Lilith as a God at the end of EOE?             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Nov 10, 2007 10:12 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: First, I think you'd have to define exactly what "Godhood" is (especially within NGE. The FAR created seeds which in turn created life on other planets. I don't know if there's any indication to think that Rei has reached that level. But I think it's possible to equate Rei III's transcendence with something similar to the Starchild in 2001 which has transcended time and space altogether. I don't know if that makes her a god or not, or just a rather highly evolved being. Of course, beings so much highly evolved than us might appear to be 'gods' by our standards, so it's not so clear.

[]: Re: Rei/Lilith as a God at the end of EOE?             Quote Post by TemporalKnight » Sat Nov 10, 2007 10:30 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: The way I've interpreted is that the Lance is supposed to prevent the Seeds from not following their goal of populating planets and then moving on (or perhaps dying). It won't allow Seeds to become "ruler" over their children, so to speak. What I surmise the LoL is doing to Yui in EoE is turning her into a Seed herself.

[]: Re: Rei/Lilith as a God at the end of EOE?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Nov 10, 2007 11:18 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Since most cultures view "gods" as superior being I'd say Lilith fits the bill. She would have done so from the very start. Even Adam's family fits the criteria. It doesn't make any of them that god though.

[]: Re: Rei/Lilith as a God at the end of EOE?             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 11, 2007 2:49 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Lance as phallic symbol into Yui's "core" transforming her into the ToL or "embryo of lives"... There's certainly a birth motif there. Now, the "why" is another matter. I think it likely has to do with some level of power Yui is achieving there (the wings of light). Plus, the fact that she now has both fruits.

[]: Re: Rei/Lilith as a God at the end of EOE?             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Sun Nov 11, 2007 3:50 pm Reichu wrote: BTW, I think the CI also implies that Shinji was a "god" after he turned into Tang in EoE. Though it's hard to tell much considering the quality of the translation...

[]: Re: Rei/Lilith as a God at the end of EOE?             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 11, 2007 3:59 pm Semisubtle wrote: After? How did they imply that?

[]: Re: Rei/Lilith as a God at the end of EOE?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Nov 11, 2007 6:51 pm Reichu wrote: Its actual function: unknown.

[]: Re: Rei/Lilith as a God at the end of EOE?             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 11, 2007 7:26 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Yeah, the ToL bit really seems to have no purpose in Seele's plans, but was rather Yui's doing.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Nov 11, 2007 7:55 pm @ the Yui supposedly being alone for all eternity stuff: perhaps some think that she'll have a "chance at finding happiness because she's alive"? But how can she become happy with only a Spear for company? ....Wouldn't becoming a SoL make some more sense in that regard? blragh.

[]: Re: Rei/Lilith as a God at the end of EOE?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Nov 11, 2007 8:11 pm Reichu wrote: It happens in association with GNK's appearance and whooshy hand thing, for some reason. Of course, I have my own conspiracy theories about that.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Mon Nov 12, 2007 3:39 am Maybe the Lancae Longinae was there to assert the divinity and ominpotence of something that created it (i.e., the First Ancestral race), or the divine Deity (or God) him/herself.

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Mon Nov 12, 2007 2:49 pm Mr. Tines wrote: GNRei/Shekinah has the qualities of a transhuman being -- most closely akin to the Star Child in Space 2001 A Space Odyseey.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Nov 12, 2007 3:01 pm Themaninblack wrote: sometimes you have to dumb down what your saying.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Nov 12, 2007 9:54 pm Themaninblack wrote: Though what you said is true...sometimes you have to dumb down hwat your saying.
